Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That: 10 Facts In Deft'S First Set Of Requests For Admissions To Pltf Are Deemed Admitted, And 20 Pltf Provide Complete Answers To Form Interrogatories And 30 Pltf Produce All Documents Demanded, 40 Pltf And/Or Her Attorney Pay The Cost Of Bringing This Motion (Santions)
Off Calendar Jun-11-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Gerald Donnellan, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Pacific Bell dba SBC
Linda Ross, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Carolyn Sisneros

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant Pacific Bell Telephone Company's motion to compel responses to form interrogatories and request for production of documents and for an order to deem certain matters admitted is granted. Plaintiff is ordered to serve complete verified responses without objections and produce documents within 15 days of notice of this order. The motion for an order to deem certain matters admitted is granted. The responses served prior to the hearing are not in substantial compliance as they are not verified. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $640 shall be paid by plaintiff only within 30 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Not reported.
